places.name: Frederick Douglass' Growlery
places.description: A stone nook where Frederick Douglass escaped the world to think, read, and write.

places.name: Japanese Pagoda
places.description: A centuries-old stone pagoda from Japan stands among cherry trees beside the National Mall Tidal Basin.

places.name: Hoff's Harmonica Case Collection
places.description: More than 500 unique and specially designed cases collected by D.C.'s "Harmonica Case Man."

places.name: Russian Ambassador's Residence
places.description: Was there a small "backpack nuke" hidden in the attic? JFK apparently thought so.

places.name: Civil War Nurses Memorial
places.description: A bas relief commemorates the "Nuns of the Battlefield" who cared for soldiers on both sides of the conflict.

places.name: Alma Thomas House
places.description: For over 70 years, this house was home to a pioneering Black artist and educator.
